requirements.title: Reproducible workspace foundation
requirements.statement: The repository runtime foundation pins Node, pnpm, Turborepo, package boundaries, strict TypeScript, generated contracts, and CI entrypoints so local development and CI run the same validated workspace.

requirements.title: Idempotent local bootstrap
requirements.statement: The first implementation slice provides a documented non-interactive bootstrap that provisions local services, migrations, provider simulators, synthetic fixtures, health checks, and safe test credentials without production access.

requirements.title: Foundation quality and provenance gates
requirements.statement: The runtime foundation blocks completion unless documentation routing, requirement ownership, type, lint, test, boundary checks, supply-chain policy, lockfile integrity, and release provenance evidence are wired into repeatable CI gates.

requirements.title: Single documentation authority
requirements.statement: Every durable product, architecture, security, AI, source, API, delivery, tooling, and status claim lives in an indexed canonical document with a unique purpose, clear authority level, synchronized routing, and a machine-readable owner where applicable.

requirements.title: No-drift documentation changes
requirements.statement: Documentation changes that alter product behavior, architecture, requirements, implementation status, source policies, model or provider claims, tooling decisions, or launch evidence update canonical docs, metadata, indexes, readiness ledgers, and validation evidence in the same change.

requirements.title: Production documentation quality standard
requirements.statement: Canonical documentation follows an explicit authoring standard for purpose, scope, authority, primary reader task, document type, status language, examples, terminology, diagrams, launch gates, update rules, stale-document retirement, source review dates, and validation evidence so implementation, support, API, SDK, MCP, and operator docs stay consistent through production.

requirements.title: Public signal source quality and citation policy
requirements.statement: Public, customer, survey, research, official, runtime, generated, and documentation-sweep evidence that affects Product Truth, requirements, documentation, launch gates, or customer-facing claims is classified with source-quality, confidence, representativeness, bias, freshness, allowed-excerpt, allowed-decision, owner, validation, and revisit metadata so weak signals cannot be promoted into stronger product or market claims.

requirements.title: Project boundary
requirements.statement: Every user-facing research object belongs to exactly one Project unless explicitly promoted to a shared library.

requirements.title: Three primary surfaces
requirements.statement: A Project exposes Chat, Documents, and Sources as its primary surfaces.

requirements.title: Opinion-informed workflow design
requirements.statement: Primary workflows expose context, source coverage, progress, next action, and recovery state so users are not forced to guess what the system knows or is doing.

requirements.title: Universal command center
requirements.statement: Projects expose a keyboard-first and visible command center for authorized navigation, search, mode switching, source-scope changes, run control, review actions, automation dry-runs, scorecards, and safe shortcuts with target, effect, approval class, expected version, cost, latency, and recovery context visible before material execution.

requirements.title: Typed command action safety
requirements.statement: Command center actions resolve through typed command descriptors, authorization-filtered catalogs, shortcut conflict checks, deterministic preflight, expected versions, idempotency, approval receipts or action cards for high-risk effects, accessibility labels, ActivityEvents, and content-minimized telemetry before mutation or external side effects.

requirements.title: Focus continuity and resumable work state
requirements.statement: Projects preserve user-controlled Focus State, Resume Checkpoints, active resource references, source scope, mode, layout, recent commands, draft references, and interrupted work state so authorized users can continue complex research across reloads, devices, Project switches, handoffs, and automation pauses without creating a second memory authority or storing private raw content.

requirements.title: Resume digest and attention control
requirements.statement: Projects expose privacy-safe Resume Digests, Focus Sessions, attention ranking, notification suppression, next-action grouping, caught-up checkpoints, and command-center resume controls derived from authorized ActivityEvents, Operations, ActionCards, source changes, document revisions, collaboration state, notifications, command invocations, automation outcomes, and Product Truth signals with explainable ranking, redaction, invalidation, and accessibility evidence.

requirements.title: Spatial workbench and worksets
requirements.statement: Projects expose a Spatial Workbench with named Worksets, task-aware pane layouts, evidence-aware splits, suspend and restore behavior, responsive adaptations, visible layout commands, and stale or redacted resource recovery so complex research contexts can be switched without tab overload, hidden state, or generic operating-system capture.

requirements.title: Projection-safe layout state
requirements.statement: Spatial Workbench state, Worksets, PaneInstances, layout templates, snapshots, suggestions, and observations are content-minimized projections or preferences over authorized Project records; every restore, API, SDK, CLI, MCP, support, or shared Workset read reauthorizes resources, labels stale, deleted, redacted, unavailable, or conflict states, hydrates panes progressively, and excludes raw content, prompts, hidden reasoning, credentials, connector payloads, browser history, screenshots, clipboard contents, and operating-system window state.

requirements.title: Multi-device and offline-tolerant workspace
requirements.statement: Projects expose device-aware, mobile, tablet, desktop, browser, installed-app, reload, reconnect, degraded-network, and offline-tolerant workspace behavior with clear capability labels, recoverable local drafts, responsive and touch-safe alternatives, cross-device resume, queue visibility, and sync-conflict review without claiming unsupported offline behavior or treating local state as canonical Project truth.

requirements.title: Policy-bound local cache and sync
requirements.statement: Local caches, service workers, offline packets, local drafts, queued intents, background sync, push handoff, device capability records, sync attempts, and conflict records are Project-scoped, content-minimized, expiry-bound, inspectable, clearable, reauthorized on reconnect, invalidated on policy or source changes, and unable to bypass retrieval authorization, evidence immutability, approval policy, abuse policy, provider policy, retention, residency, budget, or side-effect gates.

requirements.title: Optional native workspace companion
requirements.statement: Projects expose optional native and browser companion surfaces for explicit quick capture, global command entry, browser side panel or context-menu capture, OS share and import targets, selected-text or active-tab capture, scoped file-watch grants, tray or menu status, notifications, and deep links only through visible UI, explicit user gestures or Project-scoped grants, inspectable permission state, revocation controls, and accessible web-app fallbacks.

requirements.title: Policy-bound OS and browser adapters
requirements.statement: Native companion and OS/browser adapters are optional, permission-scoped, content-minimized projections over canonical Project state; they cannot perform ambient screen capture, keylogging, broad clipboard monitoring, browser-history capture, global filesystem indexing, hidden camera or microphone capture, private-content model calls, canonical mutations, external writes, publication, billing, support access, permission widening, or policy bypass without server-owned authorization, preflight, expected versions, idempotency, ActivityEvents, audit, and approvals where required.

requirements.title: User-controlled adaptive personalization
requirements.statement: Projects expose visible adaptive personalization controls that reduce repeated setup, formatting, navigation, layout, focus, notification, source-scope, and workflow-choice friction through explicit preferences, session overrides, accepted corrections, dismissals, and outcome evidence while keeping recommendations explainable, correctable, resettable, exportable where policy allows, and scoped to personal, Project, Organization, device-local, accessibility, locale, session, or memory-backed boundaries.

requirements.title: Policy-bound preference learning
requirements.statement: Preference learning records, observations, suggestions, explanations, conflicts, adaptive interface profiles, model-context preference summaries, support diagnostics, analytics projections, API/SDK/MCP views, and exports are Project-scoped or explicitly promoted, content-minimized, policy-bound, reauthorized before use, invalidated on source or policy change, and unable to bypass source authority, evidence requirements, approvals, privacy, provider policy, retention, residency, abuse controls, billing, support access, accessibility, or authorization.

requirements.title: Project operating layer
requirements.statement: Projects expose a user-controlled Project Operating Layer that assembles Work Packets from authorized Chat, Documents, Sources, Activity, Command Center, Focus, Project Atlas, Trust, Automation Recipes, outcome scorecards, Product Truth, runs, comments, publications, exports, API operations, and support-safe diagnostics so users can see current work state and next safe actions without creating a fourth primary surface or second authority.

requirements.title: Work packets and next-action control
requirements.statement: Research builds permission-filtered Work Packets and NextActionCandidates with target, effect, expected versions, source scope, progressive state, cost class, latency class, approval class, recovery path, redaction summary, and outcome links; recommendations remain explainable, dismissible, rebuildable, content-minimized, and revalidated before mutation, and repeated-work capture creates only non-runnable recipe drafts until validation, simulation, owner approval, canary policy, and outcome metrics pass.

requirements.title: Project health console
requirements.statement: Projects expose a user-facing Project Health Console that explains slow, stale, blocked, costly, under-supported, inaccessible, or risky Project state through permission-filtered findings over authorized Sources, Claims, Documents, Activity, Worksets, Work Packets, automations, policies, publications, support diagnostics, telemetry, and release evidence, with visible severity, evidence links, redaction summaries, and smallest safe next actions.

requirements.title: Safe diagnostic repair playbooks
requirements.statement: Project health diagnostics create content-minimized HealthSnapshots, HealthSignals, HealthLineageEdges, HealthFindings, RepairPlaybooks, RepairRuns, SupportDiagnosticBundle refs, and outcome observations that remain rebuildable or auditable projections; every causal finding separates observed signal, suspected cause, confidence, counterevidence, unknowns, false-cause risk, and diagnostic-waste class, and every repair uses typed inputs, expected versions, idempotency, deterministic preflight, budget and approval policy, dry-run behavior where material, Activity and audit evidence where required, support-safe redaction, and fail-closed guards against hidden autonomy, permission widening, publication, deletion, billing, external writes, raw diagnostic content capture, operating-system capture, stale support access, or stale mutation.

requirements.title: Scenario Lab and change simulation
requirements.statement: Projects expose a contextual Scenario Lab that lets authorized users preview, compare, and decide source, document, publication, automation, repair, policy, cost, model-route, repository, and release-candidate changes before mutation with visible affected resources, unknowns, redactions, side-effect class, approval class, cost, latency, recovery path, and stale-state warnings.

requirements.title: Policy-bound simulation engine
requirements.statement: Scenario simulations create content-minimized ScenarioInputSnapshots, SimulationPlans, SimulationRuns, SimulatedEffects, comparisons, decisions, apply candidates, invalidations, and outcome observations from authorized canonical Project records; simulations are deterministic-first, cannot perform hidden live side effects, label mocked or live connector tests, revalidate stale plans before mutation, and route apply candidates through owning services, approvals, idempotency, Activity, audit, security, budget, and release gates.

requirements.title: Project-level reversible work
requirements.statement: Projects expose contextual Reversible Work and Project History surfaces that let authorized users inspect material changes and choose the narrowest safe recovery action across documents, sources, claims, publications, exports, Worksets, recipes, scenarios, repairs, settings, GitHub proposals, connector actions, notifications, billing-affecting work, Activity, and release evidence with explicit target, expected version, current drift, side-effect class, approval class, cost, latency, affected resources, recovery path, compensation option, and irreversible-state labeling.

requirements.title: Reversal ledger and compensation engine
requirements.statement: Every undo, restore, replay, retry, withdrawal, compensation, reconciliation, disaster-restore handoff, and irreversible acknowledgement is derived from Project-owned reversal capabilities, recovery records, content-minimized snapshots, side-effect ledgers, expected-version checks, approvals, Activity, audit, redaction, owning-service mutation ports, and outcome observations so recovery cannot bypass authorization, hide stale state, erase history, duplicate external effects, or falsely claim an irreversible effect was undone.

requirements.title: Delegated trust and approval-load control
requirements.statement: Research treats approvals as a scarce user-attention budget by grouping equivalent low-risk approvals, proposing scoped delegated-trust grants for repeated safe work, and preserving hard stops for publication, external writes, billing, destructive changes, administration, connector widening, new destinations, larger budgets, or permission escalation.

requirements.title: Structural approval enforcement
requirements.statement: Every approval, approval batch, and delegated-trust grant is enforced at the mutation boundary with exact intent, command, action, recipe, target, payload hash, source scope, connector scope, destination, expected versions, policy snapshot, expiry, idempotency, audit, and fatigue signals; stale, widened, expired, revoked, or unsupported approvals fail closed before side effects.

requirements.title: Persistent chat
requirements.statement: Messages, branches, attachments, tool events, and streaming output survive reload and reconnect.

requirements.title: Visible source scope
requirements.statement: Chat exposes and honors the active authorized source scope.

requirements.title: Versioned intent records
requirements.statement: Every non-trivial Chat request, Research Run, automation trigger, document patch, external action, publication, export, API operation, or MCP tool call is derived from a versioned IntentRecord that captures objective, non-goals, source scope, output target, assumptions, mode, budget, risk, approval class, and expected versions before expensive model work or side effects.

requirements.title: Earned clarification
requirements.statement: Research asks clarifying questions only when missing or conflicting information materially changes scope, source authority, risk, cost, output structure, approval path, or contradiction handling; otherwise it proceeds with explicit safe assumptions and reversible output.

requirements.title: Deterministic preflight and approval receipts
requirements.statement: Deep research, saved automation, scheduled work, connector actions, external writes, publication, export, API operations, and MCP tools run deterministic preflight over authorization, source state, rights, residency, budget, connector scope, expected versions, idempotency, and approval policy, and bind approvals to exact intent/action versions through expiring receipts.

requirements.title: Canonical Markdown
requirements.statement: Documents use stable blocks with deterministic Markdown serialization as the durable interchange authority.

requirements.title: Typed patches
requirements.statement: AI changes are typed, reviewable, and carry an expected base revision.

requirements.title: Locked content
requirements.statement: Agents cannot silently overwrite locked or published content.

requirements.title: Immutable source versions
requirements.statement: Each ingestion or synchronization creates an immutable SourceVersion.

requirements.title: Explicit capabilities
requirements.statement: Every source reports stored, inspectable, searchable, citable, and richly-understood capability independently.

requirements.title: No silent deletion
requirements.statement: Agents cannot silently delete user-provided sources.

requirements.title: Exact evidence locator
requirements.statement: Every citation resolves to an immutable source version and exact locator.

requirements.title: Claim status
requirements.statement: Every factual claim is supported, corroborated, disputed, inferred, unsupported, stale, removed, or unresolved.

requirements.title: Derived material
requirements.statement: Generated summaries cannot independently corroborate their upstream claims.

requirements.title: Trust dashboard
requirements.statement: Projects expose a contextual trust dashboard that summarizes claim state, citation support, source coverage, freshness, parser confidence, rights, provider policy, publication blockers, and recovery actions without creating a second evidence authority.

requirements.title: Evidence coverage map
requirements.statement: Documents, artifacts, answers, and publication candidates expose coverage by section, component, claim, source version, evidence span, and blocker state with drill-down to exact authorized source context.

requirements.title: Hybrid retrieval
requirements.statement: Retrieval combines authorization, lexical, semantic, metadata, entity, scholarly, and code-aware methods as applicable.

requirements.title: Authorization before retrieval
requirements.statement: Unauthorized candidate text never reaches a model or reranker.

requirements.title: Capability model registry
requirements.statement: Model selection resolves through product roles and current capabilities rather than hardcoded UI model IDs.

requirements.title: Provider adapters
requirements.statement: Provider-specific model and research capabilities remain behind replaceable adapters.

requirements.title: Model council verification
requirements.statement: High-risk, disputed, or publication-blocking claims can invoke a bounded model council that records participant models, context, evidence, assumptions, disagreement states, costs, and next actions without treating model consensus as evidence.

requirements.title: Disagreement preservation
requirements.statement: Council outputs preserve agreement-supported, agreement-unsupported, evidence, interpretation, assumption, policy, missing-evidence, and tool-or-context-failure states on claims, document blocks, Research Runs, and Trust blockers.

requirements.title: Durable research run
requirements.statement: Deep research is persisted, resumable, interruptible, budgeted, and auditable.

requirements.title: Research contract
requirements.statement: Long research begins with an explicit contract covering scope, sources, output, budgets, and completion criteria.

requirements.title: Long-form section contracts
requirements.statement: Large outputs are assembled from bounded section contracts and global consistency audits.

requirements.title: Bounded workers
requirements.statement: Every agent worker receives immutable scope, tools, budgets, schema, and prohibited actions.

requirements.title: Agent development lifecycle
requirements.statement: Saved and scheduled automations move through draft, test, approved, canary, active, paused, degraded, failed, and retired states with auditable transitions.

requirements.title: Inspectable memory
requirements.statement: Project memory is versioned, attributable, editable, and never substitutes for required evidence.

requirements.title: Versioned context packs
requirements.statement: Context packs are minimized, versioned, purpose-bound bundles assembled from authorized Project instructions, documents, claims, evidence, memory, runs, and decisions with provenance, source-version dependencies, redaction policy, expiry, and invalidation rules.

requirements.title: Agent handoff state
requirements.statement: Agent handoff packs preserve objective, non-goals, plan state, source scope, touched resources, accepted decisions, tool policy, approvals, validation evidence, risks, and next safe action without bypassing authorization or evidence requirements.

requirements.title: Activity event spine
requirements.statement: Material operations, model calls, tool actions, source decisions, document revisions, approvals, side effects, automation runs, and publication events produce content-minimized Project activity events with causality, idempotency, authorization, redaction, and replay metadata.

requirements.title: Project activity timeline
requirements.statement: Projects expose an activity timeline that lets authorized users inspect running, completed, failed, blocked, replayed, and cancelled work by resource, actor, source scope, automation, cost, approval class, and next action without reading backend logs.

requirements.title: Review queue and action cards
requirements.statement: Projects expose a review queue of typed action cards for approvals, blockers, failed automation, model disagreement, publication risk, connector loss, budget thresholds, and user corrections with expected versions, evidence, side-effect policy, owner, expiry, and safe resolution actions.

requirements.title: No-drift propagation
requirements.statement: Source, repository, connector, official-reference, and policy changes create user-visible maintenance runs that revalidate dependent Claims, map affected EvidenceSpans, block unsafe stale outputs, and produce the smallest safe typed document patches with expected versions, approval policy, ActivityEvents, audit, outcome observations, and publication or export blockers where required.

requirements.title: Single canonical projection
requirements.statement: Public and private documents are projections of one canonical revision and evidence graph.

requirements.title: Immutable publication
requirements.statement: A publication creates an immutable snapshot with rights, privacy, citation, and support validation.

requirements.title: Commit-pinned repository evidence
requirements.statement: Repository citations include repository, commit SHA, path, and line range.

requirements.title: Reviewable repository edits
requirements.statement: Repository changes are sandboxed, tested, approved, committed to a branch, and opened as a draft pull request.

requirements.title: Asynchronous operations
requirements.statement: Long-running API commands return addressable Operations with polling, SSE, cancellation, approvals, and stable errors.

requirements.title: Safe mutations
requirements.statement: Mutations support idempotency and expected-base-version concurrency.

requirements.title: Controlled Project automation
requirements.statement: Project-scoped automation inherits Project context and policy while declaring source scope, tools, budgets, approval gates, stop conditions, progress events, and cancellation behavior.

requirements.title: Automation registry and debugger
requirements.statement: Projects expose saved, scheduled, paused, failed, degraded, and retired automations with run timelines, dry-run previews, cost projections, failure reasons, recovery actions, and an Automation Run Debugger that provides searchable execution metadata, redacted step traces, context-pack refs, tool and model decisions, policy checks, divergence markers, failure taxonomy, trace comparison, replay eligibility, fixture creation, side-effect safety, and outcome links without exposing raw private content or hidden reasoning.

requirements.title: Outcome-scored automation
requirements.statement: Saved, scheduled, and agentic automations expose outcome scorecards that distinguish activity from value by linking accepted outputs, rejected or edited work, stale-claim resolution, cost, latency, approval burden, failures, user corrections, safety blockers, and release evidence to authorized Project records.

requirements.title: Composable automation recipes
requirements.statement: Research exposes Project-native Automation Recipes and Playbooks that turn repeated source, evidence, document, publication, feedback, GitHub, notification, export, and review workflows into typed, versioned, reusable automation with explicit triggers, source scopes, deterministic steps, bounded AI steps, approval gates, budgets, stop conditions, output targets, rollback or withdrawal paths, and outcome metrics.

requirements.title: Recipe simulation and activation safety
requirements.statement: Automation recipe authoring converts natural-language or observed workflow intent into non-runnable draft recipes until validation, dry-run simulation, authorized fixtures, trigger dedupe, cost projection, owner approval, canary limits, expected versions, and high-risk ActionCards prove the recipe can run without hidden side effects, quiet failures, permission drift, or uncontrolled cost.

requirements.title: Automation failure recovery and learning loop
requirements.statement: Failed, degraded, cancelled, stale, quiet-wrong, or externally uncertain automation runs produce AutomationFailureRecoveryRecords with severity, user impact, source and side-effect scope, cause confidence, safe next action, retry, replay, reconcile, compensate, rollback or withdrawal policy, owner, expiry, support-safe diagnostic refs, outcome evidence, and learning links to fixtures, simulations, Product Truth, scorecards, documentation patches, or explicit non-action decisions before automation can expand or support customer-facing claims.

requirements.title: User-opinion and competitor signal loop
requirements.statement: Research maintains a dated evidence audit of official competitor capabilities and public user-opinion signals, and records accepted, rejected, deferred, research-more, accepted-risk, stale, contradicted, and non-action decisions through the Product Truth Board and SignalDecisionLedger before updating requirements or customer-facing claims.

requirements.title: AI work OS signal loop
requirements.statement: Research maintains a dated evidence audit of workspace-agent, scheduled-work, and automation-governance market signals, then records objective dimension, non-goals, affected requirements, validation expectations, and SignalDecisionLedger disposition before expanding advanced automation scope.

requirements.title: Advanced differentiation benchmark matrix
requirements.statement: Research maintains AdvancedDifferentiationBenchmarkRecords that compare accepted advanced differentiators against current OS, browser, workspace-agent, generic automation, app-intent, and agent-observability baselines with current comparator sources, screened target users, same-task baselines, good-event definitions, anti-metrics, telemetry, benchmark scenarios, Product Truth decisions, and customer-claim blockers before claiming advanced differentiation or better-than outcomes.

requirements.title: Frontier feature watch and novelty control
requirements.statement: Research maintains FrontierSignalReview records for new OS, browser, workspace-agent, app-intent, connected-app, automation, agent-observability, performance-UX, permission-governance, customer, support, dogfood, runtime, research, generated-summary, and public-opinion signals, with source quality, currentness, user-opinion synthesis, affected requirements and docs, non-action alternatives, contradiction checks, copy and second-authority risk, promotion ladder, benchmark and incubation links, owner, expiry, and customer-claim blockers before any frontier signal can update scope, accepted differentiators, implementation plans, or public claims.

requirements.title: Grounded first-run activation
requirements.statement: A clean user can create a Project, add a source, ask a grounded question, inspect a citation, and save an editable document without broad connector grants.

requirements.title: Project portability
requirements.statement: Customers can export canonical Markdown, source inventory, citations, claim status, publication snapshots, run summaries, and a machine-readable Project manifest where policy allows.

requirements.title: Effective Project policy visibility
requirements.statement: Project settings expose effective Organization-inherited and Project-owned policy for instructions, members, sources, models, tools, automation, notifications, publishing, exports, retention, residency, support access, SupportDiagnosticBundles, SupportAccessRequests, SupportAccessSessions, developer access, plans, usage, budgets, and entitlements without creating a second authority.

requirements.title: Governed administration changes
requirements.statement: Material Project settings and administration changes create versioned change records with actor, before and after values, affected resources, preflight result, approval class, expected version, activity and audit links, support access impact, and explicit in-flight work behavior before high-risk changes apply.

requirements.title: Customer-visible support diagnostics
requirements.statement: Research creates customer-reviewable SupportDiagnosticBundles from authorized Project, Health, Operation, Activity, telemetry, automation debug, incident, policy, and release-evidence metadata so ordinary support starts from content-minimized evidence with explicit redaction summaries, content absence flags, case links, expiry, revocation, audit, and export behavior rather than ambient private-content access.

requirements.title: Governed support access sessions
requirements.statement: Private-content support access requires a scoped SupportAccessRequest and time-bounded SupportAccessSession with purpose, case, data classes, resource scope, expected start, duration, approver, step-up authentication, self-approval rejection, expiry, revocation, immutable audit, break-glass review, and fail-closed guards against impersonation, arbitrary export, arbitrary SQL, scope widening, hidden mutation, billing, publication, deletion, or private-content model evaluation.

requirements.title: Anchored collaboration threads
requirements.statement: Comments, mentions, assignments, and review requests attach to stable Project resources such as document blocks, source locators, claims, evidence spans, Research Run steps, action cards, settings, artifacts, and publications with authorization, redaction, stale or orphaned state, and activity linkage.

requirements.title: Reviewable suggestions and decisions
requirements.statement: Human and AI suggestions produce typed patches or DecisionRecords with expected versions, reviewer state, accepted, rejected, superseded, or deferred outcomes, rationale, evidence links, authority, and Project-memory handoff without treating comments or decisions as factual evidence.

requirements.title: Permission-safe collaboration visibility
requirements.statement: Presence, mentions, notifications, public and private projections, exports, context packs, API and MCP views, and support diagnostics expose collaboration metadata only to authorized viewers and never leak private comments, reviewer identity, source context, or unresolved objections into public outputs.

requirements.title: Safe notifications and automation
requirements.statement: Notifications and scheduled automation respect quiet hours, minimization, explicit channel policy, budgets, idempotency, and publication or deletion approval gates.

requirements.title: Application-owned entitlements
requirements.statement: Research owns entitlement resolution, usage metering, quotas, credits, budgets, and customer-visible product access even when an external billing provider is used.

requirements.title: Minimized product analytics
requirements.statement: Product analytics use allowlisted events and classified metadata, never raw source text, prompts, generated document bodies, credentials, private URLs, or full citations.

requirements.title: Governed feedback signal loop
requirements.statement: Feedback from product interactions, support, sales, interviews, surveys, community signals, automation failures, API friction, and user corrections is captured with consent, retention, minimization, source policy, and viewer controls.

requirements.title: Closed-loop discovery changes
requirements.statement: Feedback-derived themes link to requirements, implementation slices, documentation patches, experiments, release evidence, SignalDecisionLedger entries, or explicit non-action decisions before they can change product direction.

requirements.title: Segmented research screener governance
requirements.statement: User opinions, interviews, surveys, dogfood cohorts, beta cohorts, benchmark participants, public-signal syntheses, and support-feedback summaries that can affect product direction, launch evidence, StrategicBetScorecards, Product Truth decisions, or customer-facing claims map to a UserResearchSegmentScreener with target segments, excluded segments, job-to-be-done, agency preference, AI trust posture, automation maturity, accessibility, locale, device, privacy, security, buyer, user, operator, sampling, denominator, representativeness, bias, consent, retention, claim-supported, claim-blocked, owner, expiry, and revisit metadata before stronger-than-anecdote evidence is accepted.

requirements.title: User-opinion coding and synthesis governance
requirements.statement: Raw user-opinion evidence from interviews, surveys, support, public discussions, dogfood, beta cohorts, benchmark participants, telemetry follow-up, automation failures, API, SDK, MCP, accessibility, and synthetic-planning artifacts is promoted through versioned codebooks, coding assignments, reviewed synthesis records, negative-evidence review, contradiction handling, AI-assist disclosure, source-quality records, screener links, coverage records, blocked claims, owners, expiry, and SignalDecisionLedger links before it can affect Product Truth, requirements, launch evidence, or customer-facing claims.

requirements.title: Evidence-linked product truth board
requirements.statement: Research exposes a Product Truth Board and SignalDecisionLedger that link feedback, public user-opinion signals, official references, product themes, opportunities, requirements, documentation patches, implementation slices, experiments, release evidence, and explicit non-action decisions with source, confidence, freshness, retention, objective dimension, decision state, revisit trigger, and viewer policy.

requirements.title: Contradiction radar
requirements.statement: Research detects, records, and routes contradictions across product docs, architecture contracts, requirements, routing metadata, implementation status, official references, public claims, runtime evidence, tests, and release evidence before affected work can be treated as production-ready.

requirements.title: Signal provenance and bias controls
requirements.statement: User feedback, public discussions, competitor references, support signals, analytics aggregates, experiments, and runtime evidence carry provenance, confidence, segment, recency, bias, and directionality labels so anecdotes, votes, AI summaries, or stale references cannot become product truth without review.

requirements.title: Visible performance modes
requirements.statement: Research exposes quick, focused, deep, and scheduled modes with visible source scope, expected latency, cost class, completeness, progress, and cancellation behavior.

requirements.title: Deterministic-first automation cost control
requirements.statement: Recurring and agentic automation uses deterministic preflight, rule-based routing, cache reuse, and quota checks before model calls where this reduces cost without weakening correctness or auditability.

requirements.title: Capacity and load fairness
requirements.statement: Interactive, streaming, background, research, external-write, and public-read workloads define SLOs, admission controls, fair scheduling, backpressure, load tests, and performance budgets before production launch.

requirements.title: Adaptive workflow routing
requirements.statement: Research can recommend mode, schedule, budget, source-scope, model-role, preflight, template, pause, downgrade, or retirement changes from outcome evidence, but adaptive routing remains visible, reversible, permission-bound, and approval-gated for high-risk effects.

requirements.title: Latency-aware progressive delivery
requirements.statement: Research returns immediate authorized shell, operation, and status state, then progressively delivers source scope, retrieval evidence, Partial Results, citations, document patches, exports, automation progress, cancellation, recovery, cost, latency, and finalization with explicit complete, partial, stale, queued, degraded, blocked, unsupported, or cancelled state instead of spinner-only waits or false-complete results.

requirements.title: Permission-safe fast paths and prefetch
requirements.statement: Research may use route preloading, cached projections, stale-while-revalidate reads, optimistic UI, deterministic Fast Paths, provider warmup, and Speculative Preparation only through an explicit ladder that enforces authorization, policy, source-version, document-revision, retention, residency, privacy, budget, expected-version, prepared-state visibility, hit/miss outcome recording, denial of hidden private-content model calls, connector widening, external writes, publication, deletion, billing, repository pushes, notification sends, permission changes, unbounded browser work, and invalidation checks that preserve tenant isolation, source truth, and stale or partial labels.

requirements.title: Project Atlas local neighborhoods
requirements.statement: Research exposes a Project Atlas that lets authorized users inspect focused neighborhoods, path queries, lenses, missing-link suggestions, redacted hidden-state reasons, and safe next actions across Sources, Claims, Documents, Activity, Automation Recipes, Product Truth, GitHub, publications, exports, and release evidence without rendering the full Project graph or creating a second authority.

requirements.title: Impact reports for high-risk changes
requirements.statement: Research creates ProjectImpactReports before source revocation, deletion, replacement, reprocessing, publication, export, high-risk recipe activation, retention, policy, parser, model-role, repository, or release-candidate changes with downstream effects, and those reports must show affected resources, hidden or redacted dependents, blockers, unknowns, cost and latency class, approval class, recovery path, and expected versions before material execution.

requirements.title: Content rights and provider governance
requirements.statement: Source acquisition, model routing, publication, export, and takedown behavior require explicit rights and provider-policy decisions before customer-impacting use.

requirements.title: End-to-end residency evidence
requirements.statement: Residency claims cover storage, indexes, workflow state, queues, logs, support diagnostics, model processing, backups, exports, and incident evidence.

requirements.title: Untrusted source content
requirements.statement: Source content cannot redefine system instructions, permissions, tools, or approval policy.

requirements.title: Least privilege
requirements.statement: Connectors and agents receive the minimum short-lived capabilities required for one operation.

requirements.title: Content minimization
requirements.statement: Analytics, logs, SupportDiagnosticBundles, support systems, telemetry, audit exports, and caches do not become shadow stores of private Project content.

requirements.title: Auditability
requirements.statement: Every privileged action, support access session, model call, tool action, source decision, revision, and publication is attributable.

requirements.title: Recovery
requirements.statement: The system defines and tests restore, reconciliation, degradation, and publication-withdrawal procedures.

requirements.title: Accessible interface
requirements.statement: Primary workflows, citations, dense workbench surfaces, support diagnostics, approvals, generated documents, artifacts, exports, and public projections meet WCAG 2.2 AA expectations with keyboard, screen-reader-critical, reduced-motion, contrast, non-color-only status, drag-alternative, live-update, focus-return, and accessible-output evidence before launch.

requirements.title: International research
requirements.statement: Schemas, retrieval, editing, citations, generated outputs, support diagnostics, APIs, SDKs, exports, and public projections preserve Unicode, BCP 47 language tags, explicit direction metadata, locale-neutral canonical data, timezone-aware rendering, multilingual source handling, translation-as-derived-material policy, and RTL or mixed-direction presentation without changing authorization, evidence, audit, or privacy meaning.

requirements.title: Abuse-aware product controls
requirements.statement: Research prevents abuse of source acquisition, deep research, public publishing, repository proposals, connector writes, notifications, exports, API clients, MCP tools, support access, and automation recipes through visible limits, deterministic preflight, review queues, escalation, appeals, false-positive evidence, and emergency controls before production launch.

requirements.title: Content-minimized abuse enforcement
requirements.statement: Abuse enforcement uses policy-versioned AbusePolicies, AbuseSignals, AbuseDecisions, AbuseThrottles, AbuseReviews, AbuseAppeals, enforcement actions, provider-policy checks, tenant-scoped quotas, audit, ActivityEvents, support-safe diagnostics, and outcome observations without storing private Project content as a shadow trust-safety or moderation corpus.

requirements.title: Product truth
requirements.statement: Launch claims must match verified runtime, policy, support, billing, accessibility, security, and recovery evidence.

requirements.title: Release evidence bundle
requirements.statement: Every production candidate records an immutable evidence bundle covering artifact provenance, configuration, migrations, tests, security, privacy, performance, recovery, operations, approvals, and waivers.

requirements.title: Human-AI interaction and automation UX review
requirements.statement: AI, agentic, and automation surfaces that affect launch scope pass a recorded human-AI interaction review covering capability boundaries, source evidence, uncertainty, confidence, progress, permissions, approval load, user control, recovery, accessibility, internationalization, failure diagnosis, automation outcome value, Product Truth linkage, and unresolved finding disposition before prototype expansion, beta, benchmark reliance, release promotion, or customer-facing claims.

requirements.title: Customer-facing claim evidence boundaries
requirements.statement: Every customer-facing claim about availability, AI accuracy, trust, performance, usability, automation value, privacy, security, accessibility, supportability, enterprise readiness, API, SDK, MCP, testimonials, or advanced differentiation maps to a CustomerClaimEvidenceRecord with exact claim text, allowed language, blocked language, source-quality records, runtime scope, user-research, telemetry, benchmark, outcome, security, privacy, accessibility, Product Truth, release-evidence, owner, approval, limitation, expiry, and revisit metadata before stronger-than-specification language is released.
